## 2) Frontend files changed / added

**New:** `mobile/src/api/env.ts`, `types.ts`, `authSession.ts`, `http.ts`, `authApi.ts`, `categoriesApi.ts`, `governoratesApi.ts`, `eventsApi.ts`, `favoritesApi.ts`, `bookingsApi.ts`, `usersApi.ts`, `paymentMethodsApi.ts`, `mobile/src/bootstrap/hydrateAuthSession.ts`, `mobile/src/services/api/userMap.ts`, `eventMap.ts`, `bookingMap.ts`, `mobile/.env.example`.

**Updated:** `mobile/app/index.tsx`, `(auth)/login.tsx`, `(auth)/signup.tsx`, `(tabs)/explore.tsx`, `(tabs)/favorites.tsx`, `(tabs)/booking.tsx`, `(tabs)/me.tsx`, `event/[id].tsx`, `booking/[id].tsx`, `payment.tsx`, `payment-methods.tsx`, `add-payment-method.tsx`, `edit-profile.tsx`, `mobile/src/store/appStore.ts`, `sessionStore.ts`, `referenceStore.ts`, `favoritesStore.ts`, `bookingDraftStore.ts`, `mobile/src/hooks/useCatalogPdp.ts`, `useMockUser.ts`, `mobile/src/utils/authValidation.ts`, `mobile/src/components/auth/AuthTextField.tsx`, `mobile/src/components/ui/StarRatingInput.tsx`, `mobile/src/i18n/dictionary.ts`.

Mock modules under `mobile/src/mock/**` and `mobile/src/services/mock/**` were **not** deleted.

---

## 3) Screens connected (real API)

- **Login / Signup** → `/auth/login`, `/auth/register`; tokens persisted; session hydrated on splash.  
- **Explore** → governorates + categories + subcategories + **authenticated** `GET /events` (guests see sign-in CTA only).  
- **Event detail** → numeric id + signed-in: `GET /events/{id}`; rating: `POST /events/{id}/rate`; booking draft carries `apiEventId`.  
- **Checkout / Pay** → for API events: `POST /bookings`; legacy mock flow still used if no `apiEventId`.  
- **Favorites tab** → `GET /favorites`; heart on cards for numeric event ids + signed-in: `POST/DELETE /favorites/{eventId}`.  
- **Bookings tab & booking detail** → `GET /bookings/me`, `GET /bookings/me/{id}`, `PATCH .../cancel` for numeric ids; mock bookings still work for non-numeric mock ids.  
- **Me** → booking counts from `GET /bookings/me`; profile from session/`useMockUser`.  
- **Edit profile** → `PUT /users/{id}` when `authSource` exists; email read-only.  
- **Payment methods / Add card** → `GET` / `POST /users/me/payment-methods`.

---

## 4) Screens not connected (no matching consumer API or still mock-only)

| Area | Reason |
|------|--------|
| **Search**, restaurant / hotel / experience / package PDPs | No matching public/listing APIs in backend map (only `BusinessEvent` consumer + mocks). |
| **Vouchers, wallet, membership, notifications** | No controllers for these domains. |
| **Checkout/pay** for non-API events (mock ids) | Still local simulation; no `apiEventId`. |
| **Profile / business images** | `multipart` endpoints exist for user/business but **not** integrated (picker still local-only). |
| **Business dashboard / admin** | Backend exists for business/admin; mobile hub still largely local/mock — not part of this consumer wiring. |

---

## 5) Frontend ↔ backend mismatches / notes

- **`User` vs `UserResponse`**: App `User` uses synthetic `membershipTier`, `walletBalance`, etc.; backend `UserResponse` has `roles`, `enabled`, timestamps. Display merges session user + local overrides.  
- **`EventItem`**: Built from `BusinessEventSummaryResponse` / `BusinessEventResponse`; `categoryId` / `cityId` are strings (numeric ids or names) for filtering/display — not identical to backend field names everywhere.  
- **Time slots**: Backend detail exposes **labels only**; `BookingCreateRequest.timeSlotId` is optional — app books without slot id.  
- **Bookings list**: `BookingResponse` has no image/city; mapper uses a **placeholder** image and empty city lines.  
- **Categories / governorates**: No Arabic names in API DTOs — both locales use the same `name` today.  
- **Errors**: Backend `ErrorResponse` shape is parsed via `ApiError.message`.  
- **Password rules**: Signup validation updated to match backend (uppercase + digit, length 8–64).  
- **Rating copy** still mentions “device” in one i18n string while API-backed events sync to server.

---

## 6) How to run and test

1. **Backend**: start Spring Boot (e.g. port **8080** per `application.yml`).  
2. **Mobile env**: copy `mobile/.env.example` → `mobile/.env` and set **`EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`** to your machine-reachable base URL (no host embedded in TS).  
3. **App**: `cd mobile && npm install && npm run start` (or `expo start`).  
4. **Flows to verify**: register/login → explore loads events → open numeric event → favorite heart → pay creates booking → bookings tab + detail → cancel → payment methods add/list → edit profile (name/phone) → logout (revokes refresh server-side).

---

### Security / client behavior

- Protected calls send **`Authorization: Bearer <access>`** from `authSession`.  
- **`POST /auth/refresh`** runs on **401** once per failing request; new access token stored; refresh token unchanged until logout.  
- **`logout`** calls **`POST /auth/logout`** with refresh token when available, then clears local tokens and session.

This completes backend-accurate wiring for the consumer paths that exist in code today, without adding or renaming server endpoints.

--- 713. ASSISTANT ---
Here is the review outcome and what was changed.

## What was missing (code vs your table list)

| Code | Expected table / schema |
|------|-------------------------|
| **`Favorite` entity** + `FavoriteController` / `FavoriteService` / `FavoriteRepository` | Table **`event_favorites`** — **not** in your current list. Everything else you listed already lines up with an `@Entity`. |

All other entities map to tables you already have:

- `User` → `users` + `user_roles`
- `Role` → `roles`
- `RefreshToken` → `refresh_tokens`
- `Category` / `Subcategory` / `Governorate` → `categories`, `subcategories`, `governorates`
- `BusinessProfile` → `business_profiles`
- `BusinessEvent` + children → `business_events`, `business_event_time_slots`, `business_event_photos`
- `Booking` → `bookings`
- `EventRating` → `event_ratings`
- `PaymentMethod` → `payment_methods`

`application.yml` already uses **`spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto: update`**, so on a normal local start Hibernate should **create** `event_favorites` (and add the new index / explicit column names) if it was missing. Your snapshot likely predates that or the app had not been run against that DB since favorites were added.

No extra features, APIs, or unrelated tables were introduced.

---

## What was added/fixed

1. **`Favorite` (`event_favorites`)**  
   - **`@Column(name = "created_at")`** on `createdAt` so the physical column name is explicit and consistent.  
   - **Index** `idx_event_favorites_user_created` on `(user_id, created_at)` to support `findByUserOrderByCreatedAtDesc` without a full table scan.  
   - Existing **unique constraint** `uk_favorite_user_event` on `(user_id, event_id)` is unchanged.

2. **`PaymentMethod`**  
   - **`@Column(name = "is_default", nullable = false)`** on `isDefault` so the DB column is always **`is_default`**, avoiding ambiguous / reserved-word issues with implicit naming.

3. **`application.yml`**  
   - Short comment on **`ddl-auto: update`**: local dev syncs schema from entities; production should move to **`validate`/`none`** + migrations when you adopt them.

4. **Manual SQL (optional)**  
   - `backend/db/manual/mysql-event_favorites.sql` — **`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS event_favorites`** with PK, **unique (user_id, event_id)**, **FKs** to `users` and `business_events`, and the same listing index. Use only if you **cannot** rely on Hibernate `update` (e.g. strict DBA process).

---

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `backend/src/main/java/com/vibook/backend/entity/Favorite.java` | Index + explicit `created_at` |
| `backend/src/main/java/com/vibook/backend/entity/PaymentMethod.java` | Explicit `is_default` |
| `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` | Comment on `ddl-auto` |
| `backend/db/manual/mysql-event_favorites.sql` | **New** — optional manual DDL |

1. `bookings`  
2. `business_event_photos`  
3. `business_event_time_slots`  
4. `business_events`  
5. `business_profiles`  
6. `categories`  
7. **`event_favorites`** ← required for favorites API  
8. `event_ratings`  
9. `governorates`  
10. `payment_methods`  
11. `refresh_tokens`  
12. `roles`  
13. `subcategories`  
14. `user_roles`  
15. `users`  

---

## SQL constraints / indexes (relevant to this change)

**`event_favorites`**

- **Primary key:** `id`  
- **Unique:** `uk_favorite_user_event` on `(user_id, event_id)`  
- **Foreign keys (Hibernate or manual script):** `user_id` → `users.id`, `event_id` → `business_events.id`  
- **Index:** `idx_event_favorites_user_created` on `(user_id, created_at)`  

**`payment_methods`**

- Column **`is_default`** (boolean, not null) — explicit in JPA.

---

## How to restart the backend

From `backend/`:

```bash
./mvnw spring-boot:run
```

(or your usual IDE run of `BackendApplication`).

First start after these changes with **`ddl-auto: update`** should create or align **`event_favorites`**, add the index, and align **`payment_methods.is_default`** if needed.

---

## How to verify in MySQL

```sql
SHOW TABLES;

DESCRIBE event_favorites;
-- Expect: id, user_id, event_id, created_at

SHOW INDEX FROM event_favorites;
-- Expect: PRIMARY, uk_favorite_user_event, idx_event_favorites_user_created (+ FK-backed indexes if InnoDB lists them)

SELECT * FROM event_favorites LIMIT 10;

DESCRIBE payment_methods;
-- Expect a column is_default (TINYINT/boolean)

SHOW CREATE TABLE event_favorites\G
```

If `event_favorites` is still missing after restart, check logs for Hibernate schema errors, then either fix DB permissions or run `backend/db/manual/mysql-event_favorites.sql` once (when safe for your environment).

Here’s what was completed for the device / API URL fix.

### Files changed
- `mobile/src/api/env.ts` — `getApiBaseUrl()` uses only `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`, logs once with `console.log('API URL:', process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL)`, `resolveBackendMediaUrl` strips a leading `/api/v1` so paths from the backend don’t double the prefix.
- `mobile/src/api/http.ts` — refresh `fetch` uses `${getApiBaseUrl()}/auth/refresh`.
- `mobile/src/api/authApi.ts` — paths under `/auth/...` and `/users/me` (no `/api/v1` prefix).
- `mobile/src/api/bookingsApi.ts`, `categoriesApi.ts`, `eventsApi.ts`, `favoritesApi.ts`, `governoratesApi.ts`, `paymentMethodsApi.ts`, `usersApi.ts` — same: resource paths only (e.g. `/bookings`, `/events/...`).
- `mobile/.env` — **created** with placeholder `YOUR_MACHINE_IP`.
- `mobile/.env.example` — updated to full base including `/api/v1`.
- `mobile/.gitignore` — `.env` ignored so your LAN URL isn’t committed.

### Where `.env` lives
**`mobile/.env`** (next to `app.json` / `package.json` for the Expo app).

### Where the API URL is used
- **`mobile/src/api/env.ts`** — `getApiBaseUrl()` (used by `apiFetch` in `http.ts` and by `resolveBackendMediaUrl`).
- **`mobile/src/api/http.ts`** — builds request URLs and the refresh call.

### What to set manually
Open **`mobile/.env`** and replace `YOUR_MACHINE_IP` with your laptop’s **LAN** IP (phone and computer on the same Wi‑Fi):

```env
EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://192.168.1.5:8080/api/v1
```

Use your real IP (the example `192.168.1.5` is only illustrative). Restart the Expo dev server after editing `.env` so Metro picks up `EXPO_PUBLIC_*` again.

**Note:** The value must be the **full API root**, including **`/api/v1`**, with **no trailing slash**. Simulator on the same machine can keep `http://localhost:8080/api/v1` if you prefer.

Backend, route strings on the server, and UI were not changed.

--- 720. USER ---
http://169.254.255.255:8080 this is the ip

--- 721. ASSISTANT ---
Updating `mobile/.env` with that host. Including `/api/v1` in the base URL per our setup.

[Tools used: Read]

--- 722. ASSISTANT ---
Your `mobile/.env` is already set correctly for this project:

```env
EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://169.254.255.255:8080/api/v1
```

The app expects the **full base path** through `/api/v1`, not only `http://169.254.255.255:8080`.

**Heads-up:** `169.254.x.x` is **link-local (APIPA)**. It sometimes works between two devices on the same link, but it is **not** the usual “Wi‑Fi IP” from your router. If the phone still cannot reach the backend, check the laptop’s address on the **same interface the phone uses** (often something like `192.168.x.x` from `ipconfig getifaddr en0` on macOS or `ipconfig` on Windows) and put that in `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` instead.

After any `.env` change, restart the Expo dev server so the value is picked up.

--- 728. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was in place vs what was added, and what you now have in the repo.

## Backend: what was already there

These **already existed** and match what you asked for:

- `GET /api/v1/admin/business-profiles` (optional `status`, pagination)
- `GET /api/v1/admin/business-profiles/{id}`
- `PATCH /api/v1/admin/business-profiles/{id}/approve`
- `PATCH /api/v1/admin/business-profiles/{id}/reject`
- Security already had admin-only rules; they’re now grouped as **`/api/v1/admin/**`** → `ROLE_ADMIN`.

## Backend: what was missing (implemented)

| Missing endpoint | Implementation |
|------------------|----------------|
| `GET /api/v1/admin/dashboard/stats` | `AdminDashboardController`, `AdminDashboardService` / `AdminDashboardServiceImpl`, `AdminDashboardStatsResponse` |

**Supporting changes (for the dashboard + admin table):**

- `BusinessProfileResponse` now includes **`ownerEmail`** (account email of the owning user).
- `BusinessProfileMapper` fills `ownerEmail` from `BusinessProfile.user`.
- `BusinessProfileRepository`: **`countByStatus`**, **`@EntityGraph`** on `findAll(Pageable)`, `findByStatus`, and `findById` so list/detail load user/category/governorate without lazy issues.

**Files touched (backend):**  
`BusinessProfileResponse.java`, `BusinessProfileMapper.java`, `BusinessProfileRepository.java`, `AdminDashboardStatsResponse.java`, `AdminDashboardService.java`, `AdminDashboardServiceImpl.java`, `AdminDashboardController.java`, `SecurityConfig.java`.

---

## New app: `admin-web/`

Stack: **React 19 + Vite 8 + TypeScript**, **React Router 7**, **Axios**.

| Area | Location |
|------|-----------|
| Env | `admin-web/.env` / `admin-web/.env.example` → `VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8080/api/v1` |
| Axios | `admin-web/src/api/client.ts` — `baseURL`, Bearer from **`localStorage`** key `vibook_admin_access_token` |
| Auth | `admin-web/src/auth/AuthProvider.tsx`, `useAuth.ts`, `authContext.ts` |
| Routes | `admin-web/src/App.tsx` — `/login`, protected `/dashboard`, `/business-profiles`, `/business-profiles/:id` |
| Layout | `admin-web/src/layouts/DashboardLayout.tsx` — sidebar + logout |

Behavior:

- **Login** → `POST /auth/login` → requires **`ROLE_ADMIN`** in `user.roles` → stores **access** token only (as requested).
- **Dashboard** → `GET /admin/dashboard/stats`.
- **Business profiles** → table + status filter + approve/reject + link to detail.
- **Detail** → full fields, logo/banner via `resolveMediaUrl` (prepends API origin).

`.env` is **gitignored**; copy from `.env.example` if needed.

Run locally:

```bash
cd admin-web && npm install && npm run dev
```

---

## Assigning `ROLE_ADMIN`

Login only succeeds for admins if the user has **`ROLE_ADMIN`** in the database (same as your JWT roles). Grant that role to your admin user in MySQL (or your usual seeding flow) if you haven’t already.

- Governorates: counts + active toggle via existing `PUT /governorates/{id}`.

- **Bookings trend** — still a KPI placeholder until `bookingsTrendAvailable` is true and a real series exists.  
- **“Active users”** — uses backend `activeUsers` (enabled accounts), not last-login “active”.  
- **User “activity summary”** in the modal is **admin audit lines for `entityType=USER`**, not full app usage analytics.

--- 755. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was activated:

### Behavior
- The top bar search is **always enabled** (no more greyed-out state on other pages).
- Placeholder matches your mock: **“Search available on Users & Business profiles”**.
- After **2+ characters** (debounced ~320ms), it loads **users** and **business profiles** (cached ~45s), filters **name / email / business name / owner & work email**, and shows a **dropdown** grouped into **Users** and **Business profiles**.
- Choosing a row **opens** `/users?userId=…` (user detail) or `/business-profiles/:id`, clears the query, and closes the panel.
- **Same `headerSearch` context** as before, so on **Users** and **Business profiles** the table **still filters** while you type.

- **New:** `admin-web/src/components/layout/GlobalSearch.tsx`

## Functional vs. limitations

| Area | Status |
|------|--------|
| **Events** (`/events`, `/events/:id`) | **Functional** against `/api/v1/admin/events` (list, detail, hide/show, delete, admin notes). **DRAFT** filter: backend has no real draft state yet, so that filter may not return rows until the domain supports it. |
| **Bookings** (`/bookings`, `/bookings/:id`) | **Functional** (list, filters, cancel/complete, detail). Status set may include more than CONFIRMED/CANCELLED/COMPLETED depending on API. |
| **Ratings** (`/ratings`) | **Functional** (list, hide, delete, flagged filter). **No review comment** in the data model—UI reflects star-only where applicable. |
| **Reports** (`/reports`, `/reports/:id`) | **UI + API wired**; table will often look **empty** until something creates `ModerationReport` rows (no consumer “file report” flow was in scope). |

--- 764. USER ---
Now I want to complete the moderation/reporting flow end-to-end.

Current admin-web already has:
- /events and /events/:id
- /bookings and /bookings/:id
- /ratings
- /reports and /reports/:id
- Admin moderation controllers under /api/v1/admin:
  - AdminEventModerationController
  - AdminBookingModerationController
  - AdminEventRatingModerationController
  - AdminModerationReportController

The reports admin UI is wired, but it will stay empty because users currently do not have a way to create ModerationReport rows.

Please inspect the backend and mobile app, then implement the missing consumer/user report flow properly.

Requirements:

1. Backend:
- Add/verify a public authenticated user endpoint to create a moderation report.
- Suggested endpoint:
  POST /api/v1/reports
- It must require Bearer token.
- The logged-in user should be the reporter.
- Request body should support:
  - targetType: EVENT, BOOKING, RATING, BUSINESS_PROFILE, USER, OTHER
  - targetId
  - reason
  - description / details
- Validate required fields.
- Save the report as ModerationReport.
- Default status should be OPEN or PENDING depending on the existing enum.
- Do not allow anonymous reports.
- Return a clean response DTO.

2. Backend admin:
- Make sure /api/v1/admin/reports can list the created reports.
- Make sure report detail shows reporter info, target type, target id, reason, description, status, createdAt, updatedAt.
- Make sure PATCH actions still work:
  - mark as reviewed
  - resolve
  - reject / dismiss if supported
  - admin notes if already implemented

3. Mobile app:
- Add a “Report” action in the correct places:
  - Event detail page
  - Rating item if ratings are visible
  - Booking detail if needed
- Open a clean Report modal/screen.
- User can choose a reason from predefined options.
- User can add optional details.
- Submit to POST /api/v1/reports using the existing API client and auth token.
- Show loading, success, and error states.
- After success, close modal and show a friendly message.

4. Data/model alignment:
- Reuse existing types where possible.
- If enums exist in backend, make frontend strings match exactly.
- Do not create duplicate report models if ModerationReport already exists.
- Do not break current admin-web build.

5. Testing:
- Add clear Postman testing steps:
  - login as normal user
  - create report
  - login as admin
  - view reports in admin endpoint
  - open admin-web /reports and confirm report appears
- Also list the exact JSON body I should use in Postman.

6. Final output:
- Tell me exactly what files you changed.
- Tell me what endpoints exist now.
- Tell me what to test in Postman.
- Tell me what to test in mobile.
- Run backend build/tests if possible.
- Run admin-web build.
- Run mobile TypeScript check if available.

Important:
Do not remove existing moderation dashboard work.
Do not rename existing routes unless necessary.
Keep the implementation consistent with the current project structure.

--- 768. ASSISTANT ---
Here is a concise handoff of what was implemented and how to verify it.

## Summary

Consumer **authenticated** reporting is implemented as **`POST /api/v1/reports`**, persisting **`ModerationReport`** with **`reason`**, optional **`description`**, **`targetType`**, **`targetId`** (nullable for **`OTHER`** only), default status **`OPEN`**. Admin APIs now return **`description`** and **`updatedAt`**, and add **review** / **dismiss** alongside **resolve**. Event payloads expose **`myRatingId`** so the app can report a **rating row** by id. **admin-web** and **mobile** are updated; **admin-web** `npm run build`, **mobile** `npm run lint`, and **backend** `./mvnw compile test` all succeed.

---

## Files changed (this feature)

**Backend (new)**

---

## API surface

| Method | Path | Auth | Role |
|--------|------|------|------|
| **POST** | `/api/v1/reports` | Bearer | `USER` or `ADMIN` |
| **GET** | `/api/v1/admin/reports` | Bearer | `ADMIN` |
| **GET** | `/api/v1/admin/reports/{id}` | Bearer | `ADMIN` |
| **PATCH** | `/api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/review` | Bearer | `ADMIN` (OPEN → REVIEWED) |
| **PATCH** | `/api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/resolve` | Bearer | `ADMIN` (→ RESOLVED) |
| **PATCH** | `/api/v1/admin/reports/{id}/dismiss` | Bearer | `ADMIN` (→ DISMISSED) |

**POST body (exact field names for Postman / mobile):**

```json
{
  "targetType": "EVENT",
  "targetId": 1,
  "reason": "Spam or misleading content",
  "description": "Optional extra context for moderators."
}
```

- **`targetType`** (required): `BOOKING` | `EVENT` | `USER` | `RATING` | `BUSINESS_PROFILE` | `OTHER`  
- **`targetId`**: required for all types **except** `OTHER` (omit or `null` for `OTHER`).  
- **`reason`**: required, max 500 chars (validated).  
- **`description`**: optional, max 4000 chars.  

**Response (201):** `id`, `targetType`, `targetId`, `status` (`OPEN`), `createdAt`.

**Admin list/detail JSON** now includes **`description`**, **`updatedAt`**, and extended **`status`** / **`type`** values above.

**Default status:** still **`OPEN`** (no separate `PENDING` enum).

---

## Postman flow

1. **Login as a normal user**  
   `POST {{base}}/api/v1/auth/login`  
   Body: `{ "email": "...", "password": "..." }`  
   Copy **`token`** (access JWT).

2. **Create a report**  
   `POST {{base}}/api/v1/reports`  
   Headers: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`, `Content-Type: application/json`  
   Use the JSON example above; set **`targetType`** / **`targetId`** to a real **event id** (or booking id, etc.) from your DB.

3. **Login as admin**  
   Same login URL with an admin account; copy token.

4. **List reports**  
   `GET {{base}}/api/v1/admin/reports?page=0&size=20&sort=createdAt,desc`  
   Header: `Authorization: Bearer <admin_token>`

5. **Optional:** `GET {{base}}/api/v1/admin/reports/{id}`, then try `PATCH .../review`, `.../resolve`, or `.../dismiss` with optional body `{ "adminNotes": "..." }`.

6. **admin-web**  
   Open **`/reports`** and confirm the new row; open detail and check **description**, **updated**, and actions.

`{{base}}` should be like `http://localhost:8080/api/v1` (same as mobile `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`).

---

## Mobile checks

- **Event (numeric/API id, signed in):** hero **flag** → report **event**; choose reason + optional details → success alert.  
- **After rating an event:** **“Report this rating”** appears when **`myRatingId`** is present (refetch after rate fills this).  
- **Booking detail** (numeric API booking id, signed in): **“Report booking”** → same sheet with **`BOOKING`**.  
- **Errors:** turn off server or use invalid token → inline error on the sheet.  
- **Guest:** flag on event only shows when authenticated; unauthenticated users are sent to login for reporting.

---

## Notes

- **OTHER:** no FK validation on `targetId`; use with **`targetId` omitted/`null`**.  
- **Ratings:** reporting uses the **`EventRating`** row id (`**myRatingId**`), not the event id.  
- **Schema:** JPA **`ddl-auto: update`** will add columns / enum values in dev; production should use migrations if you turn off `update`.

--- 772. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

## Cause

1. **Backend 500 on `/admin/analytics/summary`** — `topCategories` / `topGovernorates` used `(String) row[0]` and a narrow `toLong()`. With Hibernate/MySQL, `count` is often a **`BigDecimal` / `BigInteger`**, which led to **`ClassCastException`** → global handler → **“An unexpected error occurred”** (your screenshot).
2. **Dashboard “all or nothing”** — one failing call in `Promise.all` (e.g. activity log) blocked the whole dashboard even when analytics was fine.
3. **Dev setup** — if `VITE_API_BASE_URL` was wrong, the browser never reached Spring. There was no **Vite proxy** fallback.

## Fixes

**Backend**

- Safer **`mapNameCountRows`**: `String.valueOf(row[0])`, **`toLong` via `Number`**, null-safe.
- **Bookings time series**: `BookingRepository.findByCreatedAtGreaterThanEqual`, **`newBookingsByDay`** on `AdminAnalyticsSummaryResponse`, **`bookingsTrendAvailable: true`** when that series is returned.
- Null-safe **createdAt** when building user / profile / booking day counts.

**admin-web**

- **`BookingsLineChart`** (plum line) bound to **`newBookingsByDay`**.
- **Dashboard load**: load **analytics first**, then **`Promise.allSettled`** for activity log + business lists so **charts still show** if a secondary call fails.
- **`normalizeAnalytics`** so lists are never `undefined`.
- Charts use **`data ?? []`** where needed.
- **`api` client**: default **`baseURL` `/api/v1`** if env is unset.
- **`vite.config.ts`**: **proxy `/api/v1` → `http://localhost:8080`** for local dev.
- **`.env.example`**: documents optional `VITE_API_BASE_URL` vs proxy.

## What you should do locally

1. **Restart Spring Boot** (updated DTO + service).
2. **Restart Vite** (`npm run dev` in `admin-web`) so the proxy is picked up.
3. Either:
   - leave **`VITE_API_BASE_URL` unset** and rely on the proxy, or  
   - keep **`http://localhost:8080/api/v1`** in `.env` (direct to backend).

After that, the dashboard should load **metrics + line charts (users, businesses, bookings) + bar + pie**, driven by **`GET /api/v1/admin/analytics/summary`**.

### Files touched (high level)

- `backend/.../AdminAnalyticsServiceImpl.java`, `AdminAnalyticsSummaryResponse.java`, `BookingRepository.java`
- `admin-web/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`, `src/api/client.ts`, `src/api/types.ts`, `vite.config.ts`, `.env.example`
- `admin-web/src/components/charts/BookingsLineChart.tsx` (+ small guards in other chart components)

[REDACTED]

[Tools used: StrReplace, StrReplace, StrReplace, StrReplace]

--- 776. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was most likely breaking the dashboard and what we changed.

## 1. **CORS preflight (very common)**

The API only allowed `Authorization` and `Content-Type`. The browser also sends **`Accept`** (axios sets `Accept: application/json`), so the preflight could fail and the dashboard would never get data.

**Change:** `CorsConfig` now allows `Accept`, `Accept-Language`, `Origin`, and `X-Requested-With` as well.

**Restart Spring Boot** after pulling this change.

---

## 2. **Wrong API base URL**

If `VITE_API_BASE_URL` was `http://localhost:8080` **without** `/api/v1`, requests went to  
`http://localhost:8080/admin/...` instead of  
`http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/...` → 404 / broken dashboard.

**Change:** `client.ts` now **appends `/api/v1`** when the env value doesn’t already end with `/api/v1`.

In **dev**, open the console: you should see  
`[admin-web] API base URL: ...`  
so you can confirm what the app is using.

---

## 3. **Clearer errors + retry**

- Network failures now show a short **“can’t reach API”** message instead of a vague one.
- **“Retry loading dashboard”** re-runs the data load (no full page reload).

---

## What you should do

1. **Restart the backend** (CORS change).
2. **Restart Vite** (`npm run dev` in `admin-web`).
3. Confirm **MySQL** is up and Spring can connect (`vibook_db` / credentials in `application.yml`).
4. In the browser **console**, check the logged **`[admin-web] API base URL`**.
5. In **Network**, confirm `GET .../api/v1/admin/analytics/summary` is **200** (not blocked CORS, not 404).

If you open the admin UI from **another machine** (not `localhost`), add that origin under `vibook.cors.allowed-origin-patterns` in `application.yml` (there’s an example comment in the file).

---

**Files updated:** `backend/.../CorsConfig.java`, `backend/.../application.yml`, `admin-web/src/api/client.ts`, `admin-web/src/utils/apiError.ts`, `admin-web/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx`, `admin-web/.env.example`.

[REDACTED]

--- 777. USER ---
whats wrong?

--- 778. ASSISTANT ---
Nothing is wrong with the **dashboard UI** itself. That screen is the app telling you the **browser could not talk to your backend at all**.

`Cannot reach the API` / `Network Error` means: **no HTTP response** from the server (not 401/403/500). Typical causes:

### 1. Spring Boot isn’t running (most common)
Start the API so something is listening on **port 8080** (or whatever port you use).

### 2. MySQL isn’t up or credentials are wrong
If the JVM exits on startup, the browser still “can’t reach the API.” Check the Spring console for DB errors.

### 3. Where the admin app sends requests
- **Dev with Vite:** run `npm run dev` in `admin-web`. With the default setup, calls go to **`/api/v1`** on the **same origin as the dev server** (e.g. `http://localhost:5173`), and Vite **proxies** that to `http://localhost:8080`.
- **If you open the built site without a proxy** (e.g. static host with no `/api/v1` route), **`/api/v1` goes nowhere** → same error. Then set in `.env`:
  - `VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8080`  
  (the app will append `/api/v1`).

### 4. Quick checks
1. In the browser **console**, find: **`[admin-web] API base URL:`** — that’s the exact base axios uses.  
2. In **Network**, see if requests go to **`…/api/v1/admin/analytics/summary`** and whether they show **(failed)** / **CORS** / **connection refused**.

**Summary:** The admin app is fine; **the API isn’t reachable** from the browser at the URL it’s using. Start Spring (and MySQL), use `npm run dev` **or** a correct `VITE_API_BASE_URL`, then retry.

--- 800. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was wrong and what we changed.

### Behavior
The whole page (sidebar + main) lived in one tall column, so **scrolling moved the sidebar with the content**.

### Fix
1. **`.vb-shell`** — `height` / `max-height: 100vh` and `overflow: hidden` so the app fits the viewport and the window doesn’t grow with content.
2. **`.vb-main__scroll`** — New wrapper around `<Outlet />` with `flex: 1`, `min-height: 0`, and `overflow-y: auto` so **only the main area scrolls**; the top bar stays at the top of that column.
3. **`.vb-sidebar__nav`** — `min-height: 0`, `overflow-y: auto` so if the list is taller than the screen, **the nav scrolls inside the sidebar** instead of stretching the page.
4. **`.vb-main`** — `overflow: visible` (not `hidden`) so the **notification panel** under the bell isn’t clipped.

`DashboardShell.tsx` now wraps the outlet in `<div className="vb-main__scroll">`.

After a refresh, the **plum sidebar should stay put** while you scroll dashboard (and other) content on the right.

Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause
The main column (`.vb-main__scroll`) comes **after** the header in the DOM. With default stacking, the dashboard hero and other cards were painted **on top of** the notification panel, even though the panel hangs below the bell.

### Fixes (in `admin-web/src/styles/admin-components.css`)

1. **Stacking**
   - **`.vb-topbar`**: `position: relative` and **`z-index: 100`** so the whole bar (including the dropdown) sits above the scrolling content.
   - **`.vb-main__scroll`**: **`z-index: 0`** and **`isolation: isolate`** so chart/card layers stay in that lower stack.
   - **`.vb-notify__panel`**: **`z-index: 300`** and a slightly stronger border (`var(--vb-border)`).

2. **Readability**
   - **Title**: `.vb-notify__panel-head strong` uses **`var(--vb-text)`**.
   - **Empty / meta**: **`var(--vb-text-secondary)`** instead of muted grey.
   - **Item titles**: explicit **`var(--vb-text)`**.
   - **“Full activity log”**: **`var(--vb-terracotta-dark)`** plus a hover color so it’s clearly a link.

After a refresh, opening the bell should show the full panel above the welcome card, with darker, easier-to-read text. If anything still clips on a specific browser zoom level, say which browser and zoom and we can tune further (e.g. portal to `document.body`).

--- 812. ASSISTANT ---
Fixing likely causes: SVG gradients often don't resolve `var(--...)` on `<stop>` elements (browser/React quirk), which can make the mark invisible or wrong. Replacing gradient stops with theme hex values and cache-busting the favicon.

[REDACTED]

[Tools used: Write, Write, StrReplace]

--- 813. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was likely wrong and what we changed.

### Why it looked “unchanged”

1. **Tab icon (favicon)**  
   Browsers **cache favicons very aggressively**, so you can keep seeing the old purple Vite icon even after the file updates.

2. **Sidebar / login mark**  
   The SVG used **`var(--vb-…)` on gradient `<stop>` elements**. In a lot of setups those variables **don’t apply correctly** on SVG stops, so the gradient can fail and the tile can look wrong or empty—while the old favicon still shows in the tab.

### What we did

- **`VibookBrandMark`** now uses **fixed hex colors** (same as `admin-theme.css`) via `vibookBrandColors.ts`, so the mark always renders reliably.
- **`index.html`** now points at  
  `href="/favicon.svg?v=vibook-brand-1"`  
  so the browser is pushed to **fetch a new URL** instead of a stale cached favicon.

### What you should do locally

1. **Restart the dev server** (stop it, then from the `admin-web` folder):  
   `npm run dev`
2. **Hard refresh** the app: **Cmd+Shift+R** (Mac) or **Ctrl+Shift+R** (Windows/Linux).
3. For the tab icon, if it’s still old, try **closing that tab** and opening the URL again, or clear site data for `localhost`.

If you’re **not** running the UI from this repo’s `admin-web` (another copy of the project, or only opening `dist` from an old build), redeploy/rebuild that environment so it picks up these files.

**Note:** If the purple “lightning” is next to **“Vibook Admin” inside Cursor itself**, that’s the **editor**, not your Vite app—the favicon change only affects the **browser tab** when you run the admin site.

--- 824. ASSISTANT ---

[Tools used: Shell, StrReplace, StrReplace]

--- 825. ASSISTANT ---
Using Lucide’s exact bell paths for reliable rendering:

[REDACTED]

[Tools used: StrReplace]

--- 826. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### 1. **“Broken” bell**
The old `IconBell` paths were a **bad mix of Feather-style geometry** and **no `strokeLinecap` / `strokeLinejoin`**. On a 20×20 icon, the tiny **clapper arc** was drawn like a separate lump under the bell.

**Change:** Use the standard **Lucide bell** paths and set **`strokeLinecap="round"`** and **`strokeLinejoin="round"`** so the outline reads as one icon.

### 2. **Bell looked harsh / “black”**
The trigger used grey text color; at small size it reads very dark. It’s now **terracotta** by default and **burnt sienna** on hover so it matches the rest of the admin brand.

### 3. **Purple tab icon**
Browsers **cache favicons hard**, and a **JPEG** favicon is easy for the tab to ignore or replace.  

**Change:** Added **`public/favicon.png`** (64×64 PNG from your wordmark) and in `index.html`:

- `rel="icon"` → **`/favicon.png?v=4`** (new URL to bust cache)

Hard-refresh (**Cmd+Shift+R**) or close the tab and reopen. If it’s still purple, clear **cached images** for `localhost` or try an incognito window.

After pulling these updates, restart `npm run dev` and reload.

[REDACTED]

--- 830. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s the **premium login** pass: same auth flow, updated layout and scoped styles (no Tailwind in this project—everything is in `admin-components.css`).

### JSX (`LoginPage.tsx`)

```34:74:admin-web/src/pages/LoginPage.tsx
  return (
    <div className="vb-login">
      <div className="vb-login__card vb-animate-in">
        <header className="vb-login__header">
          <VibookBrandMark size={96} label="" className="vb-login__mark" />
          <h1 className="vb-login__title">Vibook Admin</h1>
          <p className="vb-login__sub">Sign in with your administrator account</p>
        </header>

        <form className="vb-login__form" onSubmit={(e) => void onSubmit(e)}>
          <Input
            label="Email"
            type="email"
            name="email"
            autoComplete="username"
            value={email}
            onChange={(e) => setEmail(e.target.value)}
            required
          />
          <Input
            label="Password"
            type="password"
            name="password"
            autoComplete="current-password"
            value={password}
            onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)}
            required
          />
          {error ? <p className="vb-login__error">{error}</p> : null}
          <Button variant="primary" type="submit" disabled={loading} className="vb-login__submit">
            {loading ? 'Signing in…' : 'Sign in'}
          </Button>
        </form>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
```

### CSS (login block in `admin-components.css`)

The redesign lives under **`/* Login — premium */`** — background gradients + radial highlights, **460px** card, **18px** radius, layered shadow, optional **card hover** lift, **centered** `VibookBrandMark`, typography, **48px** inputs with terracotta focus glow, **full-width** submit with **terracotta → plum** gradient + hover/active, **reduced-motion** and **small-screen** tweaks.

Key sections to scroll to in the file:

- `.vb-login` — page background (cream → terracotta tint + radials)  
- `.vb-login__card` / `:hover` — depth + micro-lift  
- `.vb-login__header`, `.vb-login__title`, `.vb-login__sub` — branding stack  
- `.vb-login__form` — vertical rhythm via `gap`  
- `.vb-login__card .vb-input` — height, borders, focus  
- `.vb-login__submit` + `.vb-btn--primary` overrides — gradient CTA  

Build: `npm run build` succeeds.

**Note:** Global primary buttons elsewhere are unchanged; only **`.vb-login__card .vb-login__submit.vb-btn--primary`** gets the plum-heavy gradient so the login CTA matches your brief without restyling the whole admin UI.


So all rules in `admin-components.css` (including `/* Login — premium */`) are part of the global bundle.

---

### 2. Does `LoginPage.tsx` use the expected class names?

**Yes.** It uses `vb-login`, `vb-login__card`, `vb-login__header`, `vb-login__title`, `vb-login__sub`, `vb-login__form`, and `vb-login__submit`. It also uses `vb-login__mark` and `vb-login__error`, which are styled in the same CSS block.

---

### 3. Duplicate / older login styles?

**No second login block.** A repo search shows a **single** `.vb-login` section in `admin-components.css`; there is no remaining `vb-login__brand` / old horizontal layout in TSX or CSS.

---

### 4. What should DevTools show for `.vb-login` and `.vb-login__card`?

From the built CSS (same as source, minified), you should see roughly:

- **`.vb-login`**  
  - `min-height: 100vh`  
  - Flex centering  
  - Layered **radial + linear** background (cream → terracotta tint)  
  - `position: relative`, `overflow-x: hidden`

- **`.vb-login__card`**  
  - `max-width: 460px`  
  - `border-radius: 18px`  
  - Layered **box-shadow** + light border  
  - **`:hover`** lift (`translateY` + `scale`)  
  - `::before` top accent gradient  

If you only see a flat card and **no** gradient on the page background, the stylesheet that’s loading is **not** this bundle.

---

### 5. Temporary “obvious” test style

A **thick red border** should not be left in the repo. To confirm the right CSS file is what the browser loads, add this **locally** inside `.vb-login__card` in `admin-components.css`, save, hard-refresh:

```css
outline: 5px solid red !important;
outline-offset: 2px;
```

Remove it as soon as you’ve confirmed.

**Safer check (no visual noise):** the login root now has a marker you can search in the Elements panel:

- **`data-vb-login-screen="premium"`** on the outer `div.vb-login`

If that attribute is **missing**, you’re not running the current `LoginPage.tsx` build (stale tab, wrong folder, or an old `dist` deploy).

---

### 6–7. Import path / class mismatch

- Import path **`@/styles/admin-components.css`** is valid (Vite alias in `vite.config.ts`).
- Class names in **JSX and CSS match**; no fix required there.

**Likely real issue if the UI still looks old:** you’re not hitting the dev server for **this** `admin-web` folder, or you’re serving an **old `dist/`** (or another tool’s preview). Confirm:

- From `admin-web`: `npm run dev` and open the URL Vite prints (often `http://localhost:5173/login`).
- In DevTools → **Network**, click the main **`.css`** asset and confirm it contains `vb-login__card` and `max-width:460px` (or `460px` in the minified file).

**Stale JS check:** the **old** layout used a row with `vb-login__brand`. The **new** layout uses **`vb-login__header`**. If you still see the old row in the DOM, the browser is running an **old JS bundle**.

I want you to do a full cleanup, validation, and stability pass for the entire Vibook project.

Goal:
Make the project clean, organized, buildable, and easier to maintain without changing the core functionality.

Please inspect the whole repository carefully:
- backend/
- mobile/
- admin-web/
- shared files/configs if any

Tasks:

1. Project cleanup
- Find unused files, duplicate files, dead code, old components, old API helpers, and unused imports.
- Remove only things that are truly unused and safe to delete.
- Do not delete anything that is still referenced.
- If you are unsure about a file, do not delete it. Add it to a “needs review” list instead.

2. File naming
- Check all file names.
- Rename files that have unclear, random, temporary, or inconsistent names.
- Use clear professional names that describe the file purpose.
- Keep naming consistent:
  - React components: PascalCase.tsx
  - hooks: useSomething.ts
  - API files: somethingApi.ts
  - utilities: camelCase.ts
  - Java classes: PascalCase.java
- Update all imports after renaming.

3. Folder structure
- Make sure files are in the correct folders:
  - pages/screens in pages or app routes
  - reusable UI components in components/ui
  - feature components in components/feature-name
  - API calls in api
  - types in api/types or types
  - backend controllers in controller
  - services in service and service/impl
  - DTOs in dto
  - entities in entity
  - repositories in repository
  - config in config
- Move misplaced files to better locations.
- Update imports.

4. Backend checks
- Review backend endpoints and security config.
- Make sure public endpoints, user endpoints, business endpoints, and admin endpoints are correctly protected.
- Make sure CORS works for local development and LAN IP usage.
- Make sure DTO names are clear.
- Make sure no controller/service/entity names are confusing.
- Make sure validation errors are clear.
- Make sure there are no duplicate or conflicting endpoints.
- Run backend compile/tests:
  ./mvnw clean test
  ./mvnw compile

5. Admin web checks
- Check admin-web routes, pages, sidebar, API client, types, and styles.
- Make sure API base URL logic is stable:
  - supports VITE_API_BASE_URL with or without /api/v1
  - supports local Vite proxy
  - logs the resolved API base URL in dev only
- Make sure dashboard, login, events, bookings, ratings, reports, users, business profiles, categories, governorates, activity log, and settings pages do not break.
- Remove unused CSS or duplicate CSS if safe.
- Run:
  npm run build
  npm run lint if available

6. Mobile checks
- Check Expo app routes, API client, auth, event details, booking details, ratings, reports, profile, business flows, language/currency logic.
- Make sure file names are clear and imports are correct.
- Make sure no mock-only code is accidentally used where API integration should be used.
- Do not remove mock data unless it is truly unused.
- Run:
  npm run lint
  npm run typecheck if available

7. Testing
- Run all available build/test/lint commands.
- If any command fails, fix the issue.
- If a failure is unrelated or requires missing local setup, explain it clearly.

8. Safety rules
- Do not rewrite the whole project.
- Do not change UI design unless needed to fix broken structure.
- Do not change endpoint contracts unless absolutely necessary.
- Do not rename database columns unless necessary.
- Do not remove working features.
- Commit-style changes only: small, safe, understandable cleanup.

9. Final report
After finishing, give me a clear summary:
- What files were deleted
- What files were renamed
- What files were moved
- What files were updated
- What bugs were fixed
- What tests/builds were run
- Which tests passed
- Which tests failed and why
- Any files that look suspicious but were not changed because you were not 100% sure

I want the project to be clean, professional, organized, and working.
Prioritize stability over aggressive cleanup.

- `backend/src/main/resources/application.yml` — **LAN-friendly CORS** patterns (`192.168.*`, `10.*`, `172.16.*`–`172.31.*`, etc.) under `vibook.cors.allowed-origin-patterns`.
- `admin-web/src/api/client.ts` — **`resolveApiBaseUrl()`** appends `/api/v1` when needed; **`import.meta.env.DEV`** logs **`[admin-web] API base URL:`** (already correct).

- **Admin ESLint** no longer fails on standard list-page data loading.
- **CORS** documented and expanded for **localhost + LAN** dev (still tighten for production).
- **Root `.gitignore`** reduces accidental commits of `backend/target` and root `node_modules`.
- **Mobile** gets an explicit **`npm run typecheck`** script.

### Backend: security / endpoints (review only, no code change)

- **`CorsConfig`**: uses `CorsProperties` + patterns; headers include `Authorization`, `Content-Type`, `Accept`, etc.
- **Duplicate controllers**: not exhaustively audited; names like `Admin*Moderation*` look feature-split rather than accidental dupes → **needs review** if you see duplicate mappings at runtime.

```67:69:mobile/app/business/(dashboard)/_layout.tsx
  if (applicationStatus !== 'approved') {
    return <Redirect href="/business" />;
  }
```

`npx tsc --noEmit` in `mobile/` completes with exit code **0**.

**Behavior:** Only **`status === 'APPROVED'`** from **`GET /business-profile/me`** maps to `applicationStatus: 'approved'` and allows the dashboard; anything else stays on the intro / pending / rejected flows. After logout, the gate resets so the next user cannot inherit “approved.”

--- 861. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was implemented and how it fits together.

### End-to-end flow (already supported by admin web)
1. **User must be signed in** on the app. The partner form redirects to `/(auth)/login` if not authenticated (the API is tied to the logged-in account).
2. On **Submit**, the app now calls the real API:
   - **`PUT /api/v1/business-profile/me`** — saves business name, work email, phone, primary category (ID from `/categories`), governorate, description (from “How can we help?”), etc.
   - **`PATCH /api/v1/business-profile/me/submit`** — moves the profile from **`DRAFT`** / **`REJECTED`** to **`PENDING_REVIEW`**.
3. In **admin web**, open **Business profiles** and filter **Pending review** — the same rows the backend already lists and lets you **approve** / **reject** (`AdminBusinessProfileController`).

Local demo-only delay and mock disclaimer on the form were replaced with this API flow; the footer uses **`businessJoin.disclaimerLive`**.

### Mobile changes (high level)
- **`join.tsx`**: loads categories + governorates, adds **Governorate**, maps category name → `primaryCategoryId`, slug → `governorateId`, then upsert + submit; syncs Zustand from the server response.
- **`businessProfileApi.ts`**: `upsertMyBusinessProfile`, `submitMyBusinessProfileForReview`; `fetchMyBusinessProfile` typed as full **`BusinessProfileResponseDto`**.
- **`resolveGovernorateId.ts`**: matches backend names (e.g. `Ma'an` → `Maan`).
- **Validation**: business name max **150**, description max **800** (aligned with the backend).

### Backend changes
- **Phone** validation on `BusinessProfileUpsertRequest` was loosened to a practical pattern so typical mobile numbers work.
- **Optional admin email** when a profile is submitted for review:
  - **`BusinessProfileSubmittedMailNotifier`** sends a plain-text message (profile id, name, emails, phone, category, governorate, description).
  - **`vibook.notifications.business-profile-submitted`** in `application.yml`: `enabled` (env **`VIBOOK_NOTIFY_BUSINESS_SUB`**), **`admin-to`** (**`VIBOOK_ADMIN_ALERT_EMAIL`**), optional **`from-address`** (**`VIBOOK_MAIL_FROM`**).
  - **`spring-boot-starter-mail`** added. If **`JavaMailSender`** is not configured, the notifier does nothing (no crash).

### How to hook up email (SMTP)
Spring Boot reads standard **`spring.mail.*`** properties. Easiest is **environment variables** (no YAML edit):

| Variable | Example |
|----------|---------|
| `SPRING_MAIL_HOST` | `smtp.gmail.com` |
| `SPRING_MAIL_PORT` | `587` |
| `SPRING_MAIL_USERNAME` | your mailbox |
| `SPRING_MAIL_PASSWORD` | app password / SMTP secret |
| `SPRING_MAIL_PROPERTIES_MAIL_SMTP_AUTH` | `true` |
| `SPRING_MAIL_PROPERTIES_MAIL_SMTP_STARTTLS_ENABLE` | `true` |

Then enable notifications:

| Variable | Value |
|----------|--------|
| `VIBOOK_NOTIFY_BUSINESS_SUB` | `true` |
| `VIBOOK_ADMIN_ALERT_EMAIL` | inbox that should receive “pending review” alerts |

**Gmail:** use an [App Password](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/185833), not your normal password. Alternatives: **SendGrid**, **Mailgun**, **Amazon SES** — all work as long as you point `SPRING_MAIL_*` at their SMTP settings.

Comments in `application.yml` point to Spring’s mail docs for more detail.

### What you should verify locally
- **`EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`** on the device points at your backend (LAN IP, not `localhost`, on a physical phone).
- MySQL running with categories/governorates seeded (existing loaders).
- Admin user can log into **admin-web** and approve the pending profile.

If you want a **second email to the applicant** (“we received your application”), that would be a small addition in the same notifier or in `submitMyProfileForReview` using the profile’s `workEmail`.

--- 864. ASSISTANT ---
Fixing a race where API calls can run before tokens load from storage (causing unauthenticated requests that never create server-side pending profiles), and making the pending screen verify server state plus updating copy.

[REDACTED]

[Tools used: StrReplace, StrReplace, StrReplace, Write]

--- 865. ASSISTANT ---
Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Why the admin never saw the application
The partner form **does** call the API (`PUT /business-profile/me` then `PATCH .../submit`), but many requests were going out **without a Bearer token**. After a cold start, tokens live in AsyncStorage while `getTokensSync()` is still empty until `hydrateAuthSession()` finishes—so `PUT`/`PATCH` could run as **anonymous**, get **401**, and **never create** a `PENDING_REVIEW` row. The app could still show “pending” from **old Zustand state**, so it looked fine on the phone but **nothing appeared in admin**.

### Fix: always hydrate tokens before authenticated calls
In `mobile/src/api/http.ts`, before attaching `Authorization`, we now run:

- `await loadTokensFromStorage()` when `auth` is true and there is no access token in memory.

So the first business-profile request after launch still sends the saved session.

### Pending screen: trust the server, not the demo copy
- **`application-pending.tsx`** now **refetches** `GET /business-profile/me` on focus, runs **`syncBusinessApprovalFromApi`**, and shows the **real** `businessName` from the API.
- If the server has **no profile** (404), state resets and you’re sent back to **`/business`**—so you can’t stay on “in review” when nothing was stored server-side.
- Copy was updated so it no longer says status is only on the device; it explains that **admins use Business profiles → Pending review**.

### What you should do when testing with admin
1. **Mobile**: `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` must point at your backend (LAN IP on a real device).
2. **Log in** on the app (real user in DB), then submit the partner form (categories/governorates must load).
3. **Admin web**: log in as **ADMIN**, open **Business profiles**, filter **Pending review** — the row should match the submitting user’s profile.

If anything still fails, check the app for a red error on submit or **`pendingSyncError`** on the pending screen; that usually means wrong API URL, backend down, or validation errors from the server.

## Document map

| # | Section | What’s inside |
|---|---------|----------------|
| **1** | Project Overview | Vibook purpose, personas (guest/user/partner/admin), use cases, feature matrix (done vs mock) |
| **2** | System Architecture | Mermaid diagram, repo tree, tech stack, `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`, `VITE_API_BASE_URL`, `application.yml` keys |
| **3** | Mobile Analysis | Navigation (splash → tabs → business), auth/guest/partner flows, all Zustand stores + AsyncStorage keys, mock vs API rules, i18n/RTL, **per-screen table** (route file, APIs, stores, components) |
| **4** | Backend Analysis | Package layout, security/JWT/files, **full API catalog** (23 controllers, 95 methods) with DTOs and logic notes |
| **5** | Database Analysis | Mermaid ERD, all 16 entities + fields/relationships, enums, constraints |
| **6** | Auth & Security | Register/login/JWT/refresh/logout, role rules, mobile vs admin protection |
| **7** | Business Partner Flow | DRAFT → PENDING → APPROVED/REJECTED mapped to mobile routes and API calls |
| **8** | Admin Dashboard | All 15 pages, charts (`/admin/analytics/summary`), reports workflow, notification bell |
| **9** | API Integration Map | Feature chains: UI → client → endpoint → service → DB tables |
| **10** | Missing Features | Gaps (mock verticals, no event photo upload, stale `useIntegrationMode`, security/scalability) |
| **11** | Production Readiness | Completed / partial / blocked for deploy |
| **12** | Technical Debt | Large files, duplication, stale README, unused admin APIs |
| **13** | Graduation Summary | Elevator pitch, CV bullets, demo script, examiner file index |

---

## Findings worth highlighting

**What’s real end-to-end**

- JWT + refresh (`backend/.../AuthServiceImpl.java`, `mobile/src/api/http.ts`)
- Event catalog, bookings, favorites, ratings (`EventRatingController`, mobile explore/PDP/payment)
- User reports → admin queue (`ModerationReportController`, `ReportIssueModal`, `ReportsPage`)
- Partner hub on live API (`businessHubSync.ts`, `businessEventsApi.ts`, `businessBookingsApi.ts`)
- Admin moderation (profiles, events, bookings, ratings, analytics)

**What’s still mock / partial**

- Restaurants, hotels, packages, search, wallet, vouchers, membership → `mobile/src/mock/`
- `mobile/src/hooks/useIntegrationMode.ts` still says `mockOnly: true` (outdated)
- Event photos: URL strings only; no multipart event upload on backend
- `mobile/README.md` still claims “mock-only” (contradicts code)

**Scale (from code)**

- **23** REST controllers, **~95** endpoint methods  
- **16** JPA entities, **6** enums  
- **~53** mobile route files under `mobile/app/`  
- **15** admin pages under `admin-web/src/pages/`

--- 889. USER ---
which file is has the color pallet

--- 890. ASSISTANT ---
The **main color palette** for the mobile app is:

**`mobile/src/theme/palettes.ts`**

That file defines:
- `ThemeColors` (semantic tokens: `primary`, `background`, `text`, etc.)
- `darkPalette` and `lightPalette` (actual hex/rgba values)
- Brand ramp notes (terracotta, plum, cream, etc.)

Related files:

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `mobile/src/theme/colors.ts` | Deprecated alias — re-exports `darkPalette` as `colors` |
| `mobile/src/store/themeStore.ts` | Picks light vs dark via `paletteFor()` / `useThemeColors()` |
| `mobile/src/theme/index.ts` | Re-exports palettes and `useThemeColors` |
| `admin-web/src/theme/admin-theme.css` | Admin CSS variables (`--vb-*`), documented as aligned with `palettes.ts` |

In components, colors usually come from **`useThemeColors()`** from `@/theme`, not by importing hex values directly.

## Production theme files

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| **`mobile/src/theme/designSystem.ts`** | **`export const lightTheme`** & **`export const darkTheme`** — colors, spacing, radii, typography, shadows, gradients, button variants |
| **`mobile/src/theme/palettes.ts`** | `ThemeColors` for components (`useThemeColors()`) + legacy aliases (`plum` → navy, `terracotta` → blue) |
| **`mobile/src/theme/gradients.ts`** | `gradientsFor('light' \| 'dark')` |
| **`mobile/src/theme/useAppTheme.ts`** | `useAppTheme()`, `useThemeGradients()`, `useButtonVariants()` |
| **`admin-web/src/theme/admin-theme.css`** | Admin `--vb-*` variables + gradients |
| **`admin-web/src/components/charts/chartTheme.ts`** | Chart colors updated |
| **`docs/THEME_SYSTEM.md`** | Quick reference |

---

## Semantic tokens (your spec)

### Light (`lightTheme.colors`)
| Token | Value |
|-------|--------|
| background | `#F4F7FB` |
| surface | `#F9FAFB` |
| card | `#FFFFFF` |
| primary | `#00C2FF` |
| primaryLight | `#33D6FF` |
| accent | `#FF4D8D` |
| textPrimary | `#111827` |
| textSecondary | `#4B5563` |
| textMuted | `#6B7280` |
| border | `#E5E7EB` |
| success / warning / error | Green / amber / red (WCAG-friendly) |

### Dark (`darkTheme.colors`)
| Token | Value |
|-------|--------|
| background | `#0A0D14` (not pure black) |
| surface | `#161B26` |
| card | `#1C2230` (elevated layer) |
| primary | `#00C2FF` |
| primaryLight | `#33D6FF` |
| accent | `#FF70A6` (soft pink) |
| textPrimary | `#F4F7FB` |
| textSecondary | `#9CA3AF` |
| border | `#2A3444` |

---

## Gradients (`designSystem.ts`)

```ts
// Light
primary:  ['#00C2FF', '#33D6FF']
hero:     ['#111827', '#1E3A5F', '#00C2FF']
button:   ['#00C2FF', '#33D6FF']
cardOverlay: ['transparent', 'rgba(17, 24, 39, 0.82)']

// Dark
hero:     ['#0A0D14', '#111827', '#161B26']
button:   ['#0099CC', '#00C2FF']
```

---

## Applied across the app

- **Splash** — hero gradient + white text (`app/index.tsx`)
- **Tabs** — active tint = **blue** (`primary`), not pink (`(tabs)/_layout.tsx`, business dashboard)
- **Buttons** — blue gradient primary CTA (`components/ui/Button.tsx`)
- **Chips** — selected = solid blue (`CategoryChip.tsx`)
- **Business home** — navy hero gradient (`business/(dashboard)/home.tsx`)
- **Expo splash** — `#0A0D14` (`app.json`)
- **Admin** — new CSS vars; charts use blue/navy/pink (`chartTheme.ts`)

**Default theme:** light (change in Appearance or `themeStore`).

---

## How to use in code

```tsx
import { lightTheme, darkTheme, brand } from '@/theme/designSystem';
import { useThemeColors, useThemeGradients } from '@/theme';

const colors = useThemeColors();        // semantic + legacy keys
const g = useThemeGradients();          // g.hero, g.button, g.cardOverlay
```

Use **`colors.primary`** for CTAs/tabs, **`colors.accent`** only for favorites/highlights, **`colors.card`** for event cards.

---

## Color usage by area

| Area | Background | Cards | CTA | Accent |
|------|------------|-------|-----|--------|
| Explore | `background` | `card` | `primary` | Promo/favorite only |
| Booking | `surface` | `card` | `primary` | — |
| Favorites | `background` | `card` | — | `accent` (heart) |
| Partner dashboard | `background` + `gradients.hero` | `card` | `primary` | Metrics highlight |
| Admin | `--vb-bg` | `--vb-card` | `--vb-blue` | `--vb-pink` sparingly |

Open **`mobile/src/theme/designSystem.ts`** for the full `lightTheme` / `darkTheme` objects (spacing, radii, typography, shadows, `buttons.primary|secondary|ghost|accent|destructive`). Toggle light/dark in **Appearance** in the app.

## Token summary

| Token | Light | Dark |
|--------|--------|------|
| `accent` | `#FF2F7D` | `#FF70A6` |
| `accentSoft` | `#FF70A6` | `#FF9BC1` |
| `accentLight` | `#FFE3EE` | `#FFD1E3` |
| `accentBg` | `#FFF0F6` | `rgba(255,112,166,0.14)` |
| `accentBorder` | `#FFB3CF` | `rgba(255,112,166,0.42)` |
| `accentText` | `#B3124F` | `#FFD1E3` |
| `pinkGradient` | `#FF2F7D` → `#FF70A6` | `#FF4D8D` → `#FF9BC1` |

Dark pink is tuned for `#0A0D14` / `#161B26` via brighter `accent`/`accentText` and translucent `accentBg`/`accentBorder`.

**Primary buttons stay blue** — `PrimaryButton` uses `gradients.button` (blue), not pink.

- `admin-web/src/styles/admin-components.css` — `.vb-chart-card--accent`, `.vb-notify__badge` (pink gradient), `.vb-notify__pill` (accent surface)

### Mobile components (pink-focused UI)

---

## Where pink is used (by feature)

### 1. Favorites — active heart
- **`EventCard.tsx`**: saved heart icon `colors.accent`; button bg `accentBg` + border `accentBorder`
- **`(tabs)/_layout.tsx`**: Favorites tab `tabBarActiveTintColor: colors.accent`
- Legacy screens still use `color="accent"` for price/links on favorites-related flows (`favorites.tsx`, etc.) — reads the new stronger `accent`

### 2. Ratings & reviews
- **`StarRatingInput.tsx`**: filled stars `colors.accent`; clear/report links `color="accent"`
- **`EventCard.tsx`**: star icon `colors.accent` next to rating

### 3. Promo / event highlights
- **`explore.tsx`**: hero eyebrow pill — `accentBg` / `accentBorder` / `accentText`
- **`ExploreHeroCarousel.tsx`**: slide eyebrow pills (same pattern)
- **`ExplorePromoGrid.tsx`**: tile kind labels (`Event`, `Offer`, etc.) — pink pill on image overlay
- **`Badge.tsx`**: `popular`, `limited`, `soldFast`, `exclusive` → `accentBg` + `accentBorder` + `accentText`/`accent`
- **`HeroCarousel.tsx`** (layout): kicker still `color="accent"` text on heroes

### 4. Selected filter chips (secondary)
- **`CategoryChip.tsx`**: `variant="accent"` → `accentBg`, `accentBorder`, `accentText`
- **`filters.tsx`**: quick-filter chips use `variant="accent"`
- **`ExploreCategoryStrip.tsx`**: active subcategory = pink surface (not blue catalog chips)

### 5. Empty states
- **`EmptyState.tsx`**: outer ring `accentLight`, inner circle `accentBg` + `accentBorder`, icon `accent`

### 6. Admin charts (secondary series)
- **`BusinessLineChart.tsx`**: “New businesses” line = `chartColors.accent`
- **`BookingsLineChart.tsx`**: bookings line = `chartColors.accentSoft`
- **`chartTheme.ts`**: pie includes pink slice; shared accent constants

### 7. Notification badges
- **`admin-components.css`**: `.vb-notify__badge` — `var(--vb-gradient-pink)`; `.vb-notify__pill` — `accentBg` / `accentBorder` / `accentText`

### 8. Featured / trending / limited labels
- **`Badge.tsx`** variants above on event cards
- Explore/promo eyebrows and promo grid kind labels (sections 3)

### 9. Other accent highlights (emotional, not CTAs)
Price text, checkmarks, QR icons, membership hero, business dashboard micro-bars, picker checkmarks, etc. still use `colors.accent` / `color="accent"` — they automatically pick up the stronger pink without turning buttons pink.

### Not pink (by design)
- **`Button.tsx` / `PrimaryButton`**: `gradients.button` (blue)
- **Catalog category chips**: `CategoryChip variant="primary"` (blue)
- **Main tab bar** (except Favorites): `colors.primary`

---

## How to use in code

```tsx
// Icon / heart / star
<Ionicons color={colors.accent} />

// Pill / badge surface
<View style={{ backgroundColor: colors.accentBg, borderColor: colors.accentBorder }}>
  <AppText color="accentText">Featured</AppText>
</View>

// Gradient (decor / admin badge)
<LinearGradient colors={[...gradients.pinkGradient]} />
```

See `docs/THEME_SYSTEM.md` for the full token table and usage notes.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passes in `mobile/`. If you want admin dark-mode CSS variables for `#0A0D14` surfaces as well, that can be added as a follow-up — admin is currently light-only.

--- 959. ASSISTANT ---
# Vibook Typography Audit

Scope: **mobile** (`mobile/src/theme`, `AppText`, inline styles), **admin-web** (`admin-theme.css`, `admin-components.css`, charts, inline TSX). **No Tailwind** config exists in this repo.

---

## 1. Font families

| Role | Family | Fallbacks | Import / source | Files |
|------|--------|-----------|-----------------|-------|
| **Admin primary** | **DM Sans** | `system-ui`, `-apple-system`, `Segoe UI`, `Roboto`, `sans-serif` | Google Fonts in `admin-web/index.html` (weights 400–800) | `admin-web/index.html`, `admin-web/src/theme/admin-theme.css` (`--vb-font`) |
| **Admin inherited** | `inherit` | From parent | Buttons, inputs use `font: inherit` | `admin-theme.css`, `admin-components.css` (`.vb-input`, `.vb-btn`, modals) |
| **Mobile primary** | **iOS:** `System` | — | Platform default (San Francisco) | `mobile/src/theme/typography.ts` |
| **Mobile primary** | **Android:** `sans-serif` | — | Roboto / system sans | `mobile/src/theme/typography.ts` |
| **Mobile secondary** | *None* | — | No brand font loaded in Expo | — |
| **Arabic-specific** | *None* | Relies on system Arabic shaping | No `Noto Sans Arabic`, `Tajawal`, etc. | — |

**Finding:** Admin and mobile use **different font stacks** (DM Sans vs system). Arabic works via OS fonts on mobile; admin is LTR-only (`lang="en"` in `index.html`) with no Arabic font pairing.

---

## 2. Mobile — canonical scale (`AppText` / `typography.ts`)

Source of truth: `mobile/src/theme/typography.ts`  
Applied via: `mobile/src/components/ui/AppText.tsx` (`writingDirection`: `rtl` | `ltr` from `localeStore`)

| Role | Size | Weight | Line height | Letter spacing | Typical usage |
|------|------|--------|-------------|----------------|---------------|
| **display** | 34px | 800 | 40 | -1 | Rare; large marketing (if used) |
| **h1** | 26px | 700 | 32 | -0.5 | Screen titles, names, PDP titles |
| **h2** | 20px | 700 | 26 | -0.3 | Section headers (`SectionHeader`), card titles |
| **h3** | 17px | 600 | 22 | -0.2 | Subsections, quantities, prices in lists |
| **body** | 16px | 400 | 24 | — | Default copy, descriptions |
| **bodyMedium** | 15px | 500 | 22 | — | Buttons, menu rows, emphasized body |
| **caption** | 13px | 400 | 18 | — | Labels, secondary lines, dates |
| **meta** | 12px | 500 | 16 | 0.2 | Hints, tab bar (overridden), small metadata |
| **overline** | 11px | 600 | 14 | 1.0 | Eyebrows, sheet titles, category kickers |
| **price** | 17px | 700 | 22 | -0.3 | Prices on cards / checkout (same size as **h3**, heavier) |

**Buttons:** `PrimaryButton` / `SecondaryButton` → `AppText` **bodyMedium** (15px / 600) — `mobile/src/components/ui/Button.tsx`  
**Inputs:** `TextInput` **16px** (no weight set = 400) — `AuthTextField.tsx`, `BusinessFormField.tsx`  
**Tab bar:** **11px / 600 / 0.15** override — `mobile/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`  
**Business tab bar:** **10px** — `mobile/src/components/navigation/BusinessTabBar.tsx`

---

## 3. Mobile — inline overrides (drift from tokens)

| Size | Weight | LH / LS | Where | File(s) |
|------|--------|---------|-------|---------|
| **32px** | (h1 token) | LH 40, LS -0.6 | Entry tagline | `mobile/app/entry.tsx` |
| **28px** | 700 | LS -0.3 | Language page title | `mobile/app/language-currency.tsx` |
| **26px** | 700 | LS -0.3 | Business profile hero name | `mobile/app/business/(dashboard)/profile.tsx` |
| **20px** | 600 | LS -0.3 | Explore wordmark | `mobile/src/components/explore/ExploreHeader.tsx` |
| **20px** | — | — | Signup flag emoji row | `mobile/app/(auth)/signup.tsx` |
| **15px** | 500 | — | Region picker label | `ExploreHeader.tsx` |
| **14px** | — | LH 22 | Signup terms text | `mobile/app/(auth)/signup.tsx` |
| **11px** | 600 | LH 14, LS 1.1 | Hero eyebrows | `explore.tsx`, `ExploreHeroCarousel.tsx` |
| **10px** | 700 | LS 0.6 | Badge sold-out, membership “best”, stat hints | `Badge.tsx`, `membership/plans.tsx`, `business/home.tsx` |
| **10px** | — | LH 14 | Business tab labels | `BusinessTabBar.tsx` |
| **17px** | 700/600 | — | Language card titles (duplicate **h3** / **price**) | `language-currency.tsx` |
| **16px** | 400/600 | LH 24 | Textareas, business desc | `profile.tsx`, `BusinessFormField.tsx` |
| **700** only | — | — | Logout label, stat values, chips | `me.tsx`, `CategoryChip.tsx` |

**Hero/business overrides:** `business/(dashboard)/home.tsx` — h1 + custom LS -0.6, LH 34; section eyebrow LS 1.1, uppercase.

---

## 4. Admin web — base & tokens

| Setting | Value | File |
|---------|-------|------|
| Root font | DM Sans stack | `admin-theme.css` |
| Root size | **16px** (1rem) | `html` in `admin-theme.css` |
| Root line-height | **1.5** (24px) | `admin-theme.css` |
| Weight tokens | `--vb-display-weight: 800`, `--vb-heading-weight: 700`, `--vb-body-weight: 400` | `admin-theme.css` (not used everywhere) |
| Overline utility | **11px / 600 / 0.08em** uppercase | `.vb-overline` in `admin-theme.css` |

---

## 5. Admin web — size map (rem → px @ 16px root)

| px | rem | Weight(s) | Line-height | Letter-spacing | CSS class / context | File |
|----|-----|-------------|-------------|----------------|---------------------|------|
| **10.4** | 0.65rem | 800 | 1 | — | Notify badge micro | `admin-components.css` |
| **11** | 0.6875rem | 700–800 | 1.3–1 | 0.04–0.1em | Badges, metric labels, table meta | `admin-components.css` |
| **12** | 0.75rem | 600–700 | — | 0.04em | Hints, chips, sidebar subtitle, user email | `admin-components.css` |
| **13** | 0.8125rem | 600 | 1.4 | — | Field labels, table-adjacent, topbar subtitle, chips | `admin-components.css` |
| **14** | 0.875rem | 500–700 | 1.4–1.45 | — | **Tables**, search input, body UI, links | `admin-components.css`, chart ticks **11px** in TSX |
| **15** | 0.9375rem | 500–600 | — | — | Buttons, sidebar links, dl, login subcopy | `admin-components.css` |
| **14.4** | 0.9rem | 600 | — | — | Toolbar (one-off) | `admin-components.css` |
| **16** | 1rem | 400–800 | 1.55 | — | Dashboard hero lede, html base | `admin-components.css` |
| **16.8** | 1.05rem | 700–800 | — | -0.02em | Sidebar brand, empty titles, panel titles | `admin-components.css` |
| **18** | 1.125rem | 700 | — | — | Card titles | `.vb-card__title` |
| **19.2** | 1.2rem | 800 | 1.5 | — | Modal titles | `.vb-modal__title` |
| **20** | 1.25rem | 800 | — | -0.03em | Topbar H2 | `.vb-topbar__title-block h2` |
| **24** | 1.5rem | 800 | 1.15 | — | Dashboard metric values | `admin-components.css` |
| **26.4–30.4** | clamp(1.65rem…) | 700–800 | 1.2 | -0.03em | Login title, dashboard hero | `admin-components.css` |
| **28** | 1.75rem | 800 | — | -0.03em | Stat values, detail H1 | `admin-components.css` |
| **37.6 max** | clamp(…, 2.35rem) | 800 | — | -0.03em | Dashboard hero title | `admin-components.css` |

**Charts:** axis ticks **11px** — `BusinessLineChart.tsx`, `BookingsLineChart.tsx`, `UsersLineChart.tsx`, `CategoryPieChart.tsx`

**Inline TSX:** `fontSize: '0.875rem'`, `'0.8125rem'`, `'0.6875rem'` — `BusinessProfilesPage.tsx`, `RatingsPage.tsx`, `ReportsPage.tsx`, etc.

---

## 6. Duplications & inconsistencies

| Issue | Mobile | Admin |
|-------|--------|-------|
| **Two type systems** | `typography.ts` tokens | Ad-hoc rem in CSS |
| **H1 sizes** | 26 (token), 28 (language), 32 (entry) | 28 (1.75rem), clamp 26–38 (hero) |
| **20px titles** | h2 token + ExploreHeader wordmark | 1.25rem topbar |
| **17px cluster** | h3, price, language cards | 1.125rem (18px) card title — close but not aligned |
| **15px cluster** | bodyMedium, region label | 0.9375rem buttons/sidebar |
| **14px cluster** | signup terms | 0.875rem tables (dominant body) |
| **11px micro text** | overline, tabs, eyebrows | overline, chart ticks |
| **10px** | badges, business tabs | 0.65rem badge |
| **Weights** | 400, 500, 600, 700, 800 | 500, 600, 700, 800 (no single scale) |
| **Letter-spacing** | -1, -0.6, -0.5, -0.3, 0.2, 1.0, 1.1 | -0.03em, 0.04–0.1em — different conventions |
| **Font family** | System | DM Sans — **brand mismatch** |
| **Price vs h3** | Same 17px, different weight | N/A |

---

## 7. Problems

1. **Too many one-off sizes** — Mobile has 10+ sizes outside `typography.ts`; admin has 15+ distinct `font-size` values in CSS alone.  
2. **No shared cross-platform scale** — `designSystem.ts` documents colors only; typography is mobile-only.  
3. **Accessibility** — **10–11px** text (tabs, badges, overline, chart ticks) is below common **12px minimum** for UI copy; **meta (12px)** is borderline.  
4. **Hierarchy blur** — `h3` and `price` both 17px; `body` (16) vs `bodyMedium` (15) is a 1px step only.  
5. **Arabic** — No dedicated Arabic family; long Arabic strings in 11px overlines may feel cramped.  
6. **RTL (admin)** — No `dir="rtl"`, logical properties, or mirrored type rules.  
7. **RTL (mobile)** — `writingDirection` on `AppText` is good; many **inline `textAlign: 'left'`** overrides (e.g. `language-currency.tsx`) can fight RTL.  
8. **Dead / broken variants** — `ProfileImageField` uses `variant="banner"|"logo"` on `AppText` in `profile.tsx`, but those are **not** in `typography.ts` (layout props, not text — OK if not passed to AppText; verify no invalid AppText variants).

---

## 8. Recommended Vibook typography scale

### Design tokens (add to both platforms)

| Token | Size | Weight | Line height | Letter spacing | Mobile role | Admin role |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|----------------|-------------|------------|
| **display** | 32px | 800 | 40 | -0.5px | Splash / entry only | Marketing hero |
| **h1** | 26px | 700 | 32 | -0.3px | Screen title | Page / detail title |
| **h2** | 20px | 700 | 26 | -0.2px | Section title | Topbar title |
| **h3** | 17px | 600 | 24 | -0.1px | Card title | Card title |
| **body-lg** | 16px | 400 | 24 | 0 | Body, **inputs** | Body, inputs (`font: inherit`) |
| **body** | 15px | 400 | 22 | 0 | Default UI text | Dense UI (optional) |
| **body-em** | 15px | 600 | 22 | 0 | Buttons, menu | Buttons (`.vb-btn`) |
| **caption** | 13px | 400 | 18 | 0 | Secondary | Table helper |
| **label** | 12px | 600 | 16 | 0.2px | Field labels, tabs | Field labels, min UI |
| **overline** | 11px → **12px** | 600 | 16 | 0.8px | Eyebrow (bump for a11y) | `.vb-overline` |
| **price** | 17px | 700 | 22 | -0.2px | Prices | Stat emphasis |

**Merge rule:** Drop duplicate **price** vs **h3** on mobile unless price needs tabular figures — then keep one token `emphasis-17`.

### Mobile implementation

- Keep `typography.ts` as single source; **remove inline `fontSize`** on screens (entry 32 → `display`, language 28 → `h1`, ExploreHeader 20 → `h2`, etc.).  
- **Tabs:** 12px / 600 (not 11px).  
- **Load DM Sans** via `expo-font` + `@expo-google-fonts/dm-sans` to match admin (optional phase 2).  
- **Arabic:** Add `Noto Sans Arabic` or `Tajawal` when `locale === 'ar'` in `AppText` (`fontFamily` per locale).  
- **RTL:** Prefer `textAlign` / `alignItems` from `isRTL`; avoid hardcoded `left`.

### Admin implementation

Add to `admin-theme.css`:

```css
--vb-font-en: 'DM Sans', system-ui, sans-serif;
--vb-font-ar: 'Tajawal', 'Noto Sans Arabic', system-ui, sans-serif;
--vb-text-display: 2rem;      /* 32px */
--vb-text-h1: 1.625rem;       /* 26px */
--vb-text-h2: 1.25rem;        /* 20px */
--vb-text-h3: 1.0625rem;      /* 17px */
--vb-text-body: 1rem;         /* 16px */
--vb-text-sm: 0.9375rem;      /* 15px */
--vb-text-caption: 0.8125rem; /* 13px */
--vb-text-label: 0.75rem;     /* 12px */
```

Refactor `admin-components.css` to use variables; replace **0.6875rem / 0.65rem** with **12px label** minimum.  
**RTL:** `[dir="rtl"]` on `<html>` when Arabic admin exists; use `margin-inline`, `text-align: start`.

### English + Arabic summary

| Locale | Mobile (recommended) | Admin (recommended) |
|--------|----------------------|---------------------|
| **EN** | DM Sans or System → DM Sans | DM Sans |
| **AR** | Tajawal / Noto Sans Arabic + `writingDirection: 'rtl'` | Tajawal + `dir="rtl"` + Arabic stack |

---

## 9. File index (typography sources)

| File | What it defines |
|------|-----------------|
| `mobile/src/theme/typography.ts` | **Mobile token scale** |
| `mobile/src/theme/designSystem.ts` | Colors only (no type) |
| `mobile/src/components/ui/AppText.tsx` | Variant application + RTL |
| `mobile/src/components/ui/Button.tsx` | Button text (bodyMedium) |
| `mobile/src/components/auth/AuthTextField.tsx` | Labels (caption), input 16px |
| `mobile/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` | Tab labels 11px |
| `mobile/app/entry.tsx` | Display-like 32px tagline |
| `mobile/app/language-currency.tsx` | Local 28/17/14 scale |
| `mobile/src/components/explore/ExploreHeader.tsx` | 20px / 15px wordmark |
| `admin-web/index.html` | **DM Sans import** |
| `admin-web/src/theme/admin-theme.css` | Root font, 16px base, overline |
| `admin-web/src/styles/admin-components.css` | **~100 typography rules** (buttons, tables, sidebar, dashboard, login, modals) |
| `admin-web/src/components/charts/*.tsx` | Chart tick 11px |

---

## 10. Quick wins (priority)

1. Unify mobile tabs + overline to **12px** minimum.  
2. Replace mobile one-offs with `AppText` variants (entry, language screen, Explore header).  
3. Add `--vb-text-*` CSS variables and map admin classes to a **9-step scale**.

--- 964. ASSISTANT ---
Typography refactor is complete across mobile and admin. Summary:

## Unified system

- **Mobile:** `mobile/src/theme/typography.ts` is the source of truth; `AppText` applies tokens + **DM Sans** (EN) / **Tajawal** (AR).
- **Admin:** `admin-web/src/theme/admin-theme.css` defines `--vb-text-display` through `--vb-text-label`; `admin-components.css` uses those variables instead of scattered rem values.

## Mobile fixes

- Removed **10px / 11px / 14px / 28px** inline sizes; mapped to scale variants (`display`, `h1`, `h2`, `h3`, `caption`, `overline`, `label`).
- **ExploreHeader**, **language-currency**, tabs, badges, and business tab bar now use `AppText`.
- **Text inputs** use `inputTextStyle(locale)` (and `h1` for profile name).
- **RTL:** `textAlignStart(isRTL)` on me guest + language screens; hardcoded `textAlign: 'left'` removed except phone LTR fields (+962).
- **Tab bar:** 12px/600 + locale `fontFamily`.

## Admin fixes

- Typography CSS variables added; micro text **0.65–0.6875rem** bumped to **label (12px)**.
- **Tajawal** loaded; `html[dir='rtl']` uses Arabic stack.

## Audit complete
